arts.ttl: Koopmeiners and Nico Gonzalez set to miss Inter match, tests for Douglas Luiz
arts.sum: Juventus are preparing to face Inter Milan at San Siro amid an injury crisis, but there’s a glimmer of hope regarding Douglas Luiz’s condition. Thiago Motta’s squad is dealing with numerous absences, and the recent match against Stuttgart has left its mark. Not only did it result in their first Champions League and overall seasonal defeat, but it also saw the Brazilian midfielder sidelined just before the game at the Allianz Stadium. “He experienced some minor discomfort, and as a precaution, the medical staff decided to rest him. It doesn’t seem serious, but it’s certainly not what we needed,” Giuntoli The post Koopmeiners and Nico Gonzalez set to miss Inter match, tests for Douglas Luiz appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Champions League | Young Boys v Inter: Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: Inter Milan, the 2022/23 Champions League finalists, are looking to maintain their momentum with a fourth consecutive victory as they prepare to face Young Boys in the prestigious tournament on Wednesday. The Nerazzurri began their campaign with a goalless draw against Manchester City, a rematch of the final they lost two seasons ago. They followed that up with a commanding 4-0 win over Red Star Belgrade and secured two Serie A victories against Torino and Roma, significantly boosting their confidence ahead of this match. arts.ttl: Inter Milan’s new midfield target: Gianluca Busio in Oaktree’s plans
arts.sum: Inter Milan are embarking on a rejuvenation strategy under the guidance of Oaktree Capital, an approach that will impact every department of the team. This strategic shift aims to strengthen the squad available to coach Simone Inzaghi, with club president Giuseppe Marotta and sporting director Piero Ausilio leading the charge. La Stampa (via Tribuna) report that one of the primary targets in this new strategy is Gianluca Busio, a young American midfielder currently playing for Venezia. arts.ttl: Gazzetta: New San Siro stadium is the best solution for Inter and Milan
arts.sum: Inter and AC Milan have come full circle, returning to their original plan from five years ago: building a new stadium in the San Siro area. This decision marks a significant shift from recent proposals to relocate to San Donato or Sesto San Giovanni, and it’s a move that makes sense for several reasons. La Gazzetta dello Sport report how the San Siro area has long been synonymous with sports in Milan.
